key_metrics: Billed business of $428.0 billion, up 10% YoY
Total Card balances and Other loans of $224.16 billion, up 8% YoY
Provisions for credit losses of $1.25 billion, up 9% YoY
Net write-off rate (principal, interest and fees) of 2.3%
qoq_changes: Sequential decrease in Card balances driving a reserve release
Softening of airline spend in the last few weeks of the quarter
new_risks: Travel disruptions and softening airline spend due to the Middle East conflict
Uncertainty in the macroeconomic, geopolitical, and regulatory landscape
Higher net write-offs and lower reserve releases impacting credit loss provisions
management_tone: Bullish, as management highlighted strong engagement on refreshed premium products, accelerated billed business growth, and confidence in navigating evolving economic environments.
analyst_note: American Express posted a strong Q1 2026 with double-digit top and bottom-line growth fueled by Millennial/Gen-Z spending and international expansion. However, rising credit provisions and late-quarter travel softening warrant close monitoring in the near term.
